mentions.note: At 42x earnings the multiple is still too high given software's struggle to keep pace with AI-driven hardware demand.
Laid to waste with other software names on fears AI will replace their business.
Chart is too weak to call a bottom despite the one-year low.
Alongside Salesforce, SaaS leaders; both doing well but stocks not playing ball. NOW down 100+ pts from high. Bill McDermott must explain if broken stock vs broken company.

Stock down 45% from peak but Q4 beat; $5B buyback ($2B accelerated) signals management conviction. AI not eroding software; consolidating feature companies. Rule of 57 metrics impressive; partnership with Anthropic validates platform approach.
Multiple compression, not earnings failure; down 50% YoY, P/E collapsed 65× to 28×; market fears AI will reduce per-user value; wait for multiple bottom before buying despite excellent business.
Down 7% on cloud workflow platform disruption fears. Avoid catching this falling knife.
Down 50%; 19% earnings growth expected; down from 70x forward multiples to 24x; $2B accelerated buyback announced.
Specifically targeted in Citrini Research AI apocalypse paper; facing multiple compression on fears of client self-sufficiency with AI.
Soared 3.5% today as enterprise software quartet rallies; AI code-writing threat overstated vs. actual LBO protection.
Enterprise software bounce long overdue; CEOs pivoting to combat AI competition; oversold on apocalypse narrative.
Sells at 26x earnings—cheap for great growth—but SaaS sector disliked intensely; expect more turbulence despite respecting CEO Bill McDermot.
Enterprise software incumbent vulnerable to AI disruption; Cramer notes these stocks were 'exalted winners' but now face predatory competition from OpenAI, Anthropic.
Enterprise software down nearly 8% in IGV selloff; hardware dominance persisting.
Expanded buyback, CEO buying at higher prices, but still down 42% YTD; mixed disruptable and non-disruptable divisions — wait for clarity.
Rotation play; soared from depressed levels amid enterprise software rebound; no fundamental news, pure sector rotation.
Anthropic disruption fears suppressing stock despite solid fundamentals; good news may finally matter but disruption narrative persists.

Crushed from $240 peak to $103 on AI displacement worries; beat & raise quarter with $1.5B AI revenue guidance, rule-of-60 trajectory, but market skeptical on valuation; cheapness eventual strength if AI tailwinds materialize.

Excellent quarter (Rule of 50 pass, strong AI adoption) but down 44% YTD; enterprise software pricing under pressure from AI; trading 37x earnings even after 17% crash; won't get premium multiple in disruption era.
Hammered on AI displacement worries dragging SaaS; analyst meeting Monday may clarify contract length trends; software group fired today—positive signal for recovery.
Software sector despised; solid company facing market headwinds.
Analyst meeting halo lasted only 24 hours; failed to generate lasting market enthusiasm like Data Dog did.
Part of software rebound group delivering magnificent performances after month-long punishment; data center tailwinds reasserting
Expensive SaaS stock with ongoing pivot. Better than most enterprise software peers but not time to buy in cautious environment. Cramer has mixed feelings.
Down 31% YTD, AI platform headwinds from Anthropic Claude and OpenAI eating seats; couple-day bounce likely but enterprise software group needs earnings catalyst for durability.
Beat on every key line with strong guidance but fell 10%; multiple compression severe (64x→28x earnings); demonstrates market doesn't value numbers when industry credibility questioned.
Recommended alongside CRM for enterprise software believers, but massive January losses and sector weakness argue against aggressive positioning.
Jumped 5% in software rally but gains likely temporary; history shows enterprise software moves meet torrential selling after initial strength.
